name: Davis minimal medium + 100 ug/L glucose (DM100) (PMC2872623)
description: Davis minimal medium + 100 ug/L glucose (DM100) — medium reported in PMC2872623 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC2872623 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Complex medium: chemically-defined portion mapped to BiGG; biological extracts listed in `unmapped`. Added via GrowthDB curation.
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.base_expansion: Base 'Davis' expanded to standard composition: Davis minimal medium (Davis & Mingioli 1950): K2HPO4/KH2PO4, (NH4)2SO4, Na-citrate, MgSO4 + glucose.
provenance.verification_evidence: K2HPO4: 5.34 g (MW 174.18) ... Add deionized water to final volume and autoclave (Barrick lab Davis-Mingioli DM recipe reproducing the Lenski et al. 1991 medium).
provenance.base_medium: Davis (Davis-Mingioli) minimal medium as used in the E. coli long-term evolution experiment (LTEE), glucose-limited, with thiamine

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as 'Davis minimal media (Lenski et al. 1991)', Am Nat 138:1315-1341 (ref bib25)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch barricklab.org Davis-Mingioli DM protocol (reproduces the exact Lenski/LTEE recipe) + WebSearch protocols.io/OpenWetWare cross-check

missing_before: Trisodium citrate dihydrate (sodium citrate) — provides citrate + Na (was in current_uncovered)
Dipotassium/monopotassium phosphate and MgSO4·7H2O were present only as free ions in the model exchange list; now resolved to the actual named salts with concentrations
recuration.notes: Complete defined recipe recovered from the LTEE Davis-Mingioli base medium (Lenski et al. 1991) plus the paper's DM100 glucose modification. Davis minimal medium is a simple defined medium with NO trace-element or multi-vitamin solution added: the open EX_ca2/cobalt2/cu2/fe2/fe3/mn2/zn2 exchanges in current_components are model defaults / trace reagent contaminants and are NOT deliberately-added recipe ingredients (no textual source supports them), so they are excluded from the recipe above. Cultures were grown aerobically (37 C, orbital shaking at 160 rpm in test tubes), so oxygen corrected f
